name: Recursive DFS
summary: Swap each node's two children, then recurse into both subtrees.
visualizationIntent: init: build and show the input tree from the level-order array; every node idle. recurse: a cursor ring lands on the current node (node_id); its two child subtrees swap positions (left<->right) with a glide, so the mirror is visible; ancestors still on the call stack stay amber (frontier), fully-returned subtrees go blue (visited). move: cursor descends into the (now-swapped) left subtree, then the right — depth-first, so one whole side mirrors before the other. return: serialize the fully mirrored tree back to level-order into the result strip.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many array slots describe the tree.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty list of real node objects for the drawing.
lineExplanations.5: Start a lookup from a node's id to its object.
lineExplanations.6: Start a per-node state tracker: unseen, on-stack, or done.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
lineExplanations.9: Point the child-slot walker just past the root.
lineExplanations.10: Loop over every slot to build the node objects.
lineExplanations.11: Only non-null slots become real nodes.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node with its value and two empty child links.
lineExplanations.13: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
lineExplanations.14: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.15: Link that slot as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.16: Advance the walker past the left-child slot.
lineExplanations.17: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
lineExplanations.18: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.19: Link that slot as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the walker past the right-child slot.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the tree.
lineExplanations.22: Record the node under its id for O(1) lookup.
lineExplanations.23: The root is slot 0, or nothing when the tree is empty.
lineExplanations.24: Recursively invert the whole tree, starting at the root.
lineExplanations.25: Start an empty output list for the level-order result.
lineExplanations.26: Start a queue to walk the inverted tree for serialization.
lineExplanations.27: Seed the walk with the root when the tree is non-empty.
lineExplanations.28: Put the root id into the serialization queue.
lineExplanations.29: Keep emitting while ids remain to serialize.
lineExplanations.30: Take the next id from the front of the walk.
lineExplanations.31: A None entry marks a missing child.
lineExplanations.32: Emit null for that missing child.
lineExplanations.34: Emit this node's value into the result.
lineExplanations.35: Queue its left child to emit next.
lineExplanations.36: Queue its right child after that.
lineExplanations.37: Trim trailing nulls the level-order format omits.
lineExplanations.39: Return the inverted tree in level-order form.
lineExplanations.42: Base case: a missing node — nothing to invert.
lineExplanations.43: Return immediately for a null child.
lineExplanations.44: Mark this node active — its frame is now on the call stack.
lineExplanations.47: Make the old right child the new left child.
lineExplanations.48: Make the old left child the new right child.
lineExplanations.49: Recurse into the now-swapped left subtree.
lineExplanations.50: Recurse into the now-swapped right subtree.
lineExplanations.51: Both subtrees done — mark this node fully inverted.
lineExplanations.52: Return control to the parent frame.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def invertTree(self, values): the entry method; values is the tree as a level-order array.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(values) counts the elements in the input array.
syntaxExplanations.5: by_id = {} creates an empty dictionary (a map from id to node).
syntaxExplanations.6: state_by_id = [] will hold one status number per node id.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: append(0) adds a 0 (unseen) entry for this slot.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 sets the next-child pointer to slot 1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): a second pass, this time to build nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: is not None checks the slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal { ... } builds this node object.
syntaxExplanations.13: if child < n: is there still a slot available?
syntaxExplanations.14: Check the candidate child slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child stores the left child's id.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 advances to the next slot.
syntaxExplanations.17: if child < n: is there another slot for the right child?
syntaxExplanations.18: Check the candidate right slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child stores the right child's id.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds the built node to the list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node registers the node under its id.
syntaxExplanations.23: A ? B : C-style conditional: 0 if there is a root, else None.
syntaxExplanations.24: self.invert(...) calls the recursive helper on the root.
syntaxExplanations.25: result = [] will collect the level-order output.
syntaxExplanations.26: out_q = [] is a list used as a queue.
syntaxExplanations.27: if root is not None: only walk a non-empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.29: while len(out_q) > 0: keep going until the queue empties.
syntaxExplanations.30: pop(0) removes and returns the front item (dequeue).
syntaxExplanations.31: if emit_id is None: this slot is a missing child.
syntaxExplanations.32: append(None) records a null hole in the output.
syntaxExplanations.33: else: the slot is a real node.
syntaxExplanations.35: append(..."left"...) enqueues the left child id.
syntaxExplanations.36: append(..."right"...) enqueues the right child id.
syntaxExplanations.37: while ... is None: trim trailing null placeholders.
syntaxExplanations.39: return result hands back the inverted tree.
syntaxExplanations.41: def invert(self, node_id, nodes, by_id, state_by_id): the recursive helper; extra args are passed so the visualization can see them each frame.
syntaxExplanations.42: if node_id is None: the base case — an absent child.
syntaxExplanations.43: return with no value exits this call.
syntaxExplanations.45: Read the current left-child id into left.
syntaxExplanations.46: Read the current right-child id into right.
syntaxExplanations.47: Assign right into the left slot.
syntaxExplanations.48: Assign left into the right slot — the swap is done.
syntaxExplanations.49: self.invert(...) recurses on the new left subtree.
syntaxExplanations.50: self.invert(...) recurses on the new right subtree.
syntaxExplanations.51: state_by_id[node_id] = 2 marks the node fully done.
